title: Dicliptera decorticans
taxonomy: Dicliptera decorticans belongs to the family Acanthaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is native to tropical regions and is classified within the genus Dicliptera. Dicliptera decorticans is a member of the order Lamiales and is closely related to other flowering plants in this order. The classification of Dicliptera decorticans also includes its classification as a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two cotyledons in its seedling stage. This classification helps to identify and understand the evolutionary relationships of Dicliptera decorticans with other plants.

intro: Dicliptera decorticans is a plant species commonly known as "Guaco" or "Mexican Yerba Santa," belonging to the Acanthaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions like wounds and acne. The bioactive constituents of Dicliptera decorticans include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and as a natural expectorant.
